title: Constraint conservation salt-only control

queries: task selective graph transfer negative transfer disable source path ablation
domain selective prompt transfer conservative graph alignment control ablation
when to disable transfer branch to avoid negative transfer structured representation alignment

evidence.why_now: EXP201 removed the new Amazon-side conservation penalties and pulled both Amazon tasks back to screening-neutral while preserving the strong salt-side continuation signal, but it still did not create positive Amazon evidence. The next justified causal control is therefore to test whether the remaining Amazon harm comes from any Amazon-side transfer at all: keep the validated Part 1 base on Amazon, keep the stronger salt-side Part 3 path unchanged, and measure whether task-selective enablement is the cleaner continuation.

hypothesis: If the useful Part 3 mechanism is genuinely task-selective and the remaining Amazon regression after EXP201 comes from graph-to-token transfer itself rather than from the already-removed Amazon-side conservation losses, then disabling Amazon-side residual and graph injection entirely while preserving the strong salt-side configuration should recover the validated Amazon baseline behavior without sacrificing the salt-side gain signal.
notes: This is a screening-only causal control after EXP201 showed that removing Amazon-side conservation penalties restores both Amazon tasks to screening-neutral without breaking the salt-side signal.
The intended split is stricter than EXP201: Amazon-side Part 3 transfer itself is disabled, while the validated salt-side Part 3 path stays unchanged.
Do not run Optuna or final full test from this candidate directly.
The comparison remains fair because the validated Phase 2 per-task best hyperparameters stay fixed and only the task-selective Part 3 transfer enablement changes.

Scientific interpretation still follows the Stage 3 task-aware rule set: user-ltv subset/full-test scale mismatch still applies, and item-incoterms should still be judged scientifically against the stored full-test reference band rather than only the optimistic subset baseline.
